# Chapter 13 Floor Staff Workbook

Use the Floor Staff feature to track the personnel and equipment assigned to the casino floor.
Each employee and piece of equipment is entered into the system and assigned a unique
identification number. Employees receive certain rights within the system. Each employee
using a piece of equipment is tracked based on their equipment rights. IAttendant Workstation
shares this information.


## 13.1 Floor Staff Functions

Complete the following floor-staff functions in the Floor Staff Workbook window:

· Activate and deactivate floor staff

· Promote and demote individuals or groups of floor staff

· Change the status of the employee (ON or OFF shift)

· Set pager options (Always On Shift or Clear Always On Shift)

· Check equipment IN and OUT

· Print barcode labels for attaching to equipment

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="177" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 13 Floor Staff Workbook" -->


## 13.2 Accessing the Floor Staff Workbook

To access the Floor Staff Workbook:

Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Floor Staff Information. The Floor Staff Workbook
window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/178.1.png)




### To filter the Floor Staff pane:

Select from the following options, then click Update:

· Active-Displays only active staff.

· Promoted-Displays only promoted staff.

· On Shift-Displays only staff currently On Shift.

· Paging - Always On Shift-Displays only staff with the Paging - Always On Shift
feature activated.

· Promote Eligible-Displays only staff eligible for promotion during the current shift.

· Demote Eligible-Displays only staff promoted during the current shift.

Use the following table to interpret the colors in the Floor Staff Workbook:


<table>
<tr>
<th>Color</th>
<th>Description</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Green</td>
<td>Currently ON SHIFT.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Red</td>
<td>ON SHIFT longer than eight hours.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Yellow</td>
<td>Not activated.</td>
</tr>
</table>


## 13.3 Activating Floor Staff

Add users to the system in UMS (with UMS integration enabled) or in the Maintenance
Console (with UMS integration disabled. Activated users before they are placed ON Shift or
issued equipment.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="178" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 13 Floor Staff Workbook" -->

To activate floor staff:




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/179.1.png)




Inactive users are highlighted in yellow; active users are highlighted in white.

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Floor Staff Information. The Floor Staff
Workbook window appears.

2\. Select the staff member to activate.
OR
Press and hold CTRL to select multiple members in the Floor Staff Workbook.

3\. Click Activate.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/179.2.png)




The selected user is no longer highlighted yellow.


## 13.4 Deactivating Floor Staff

A user may be deactivated regardless of status. For example, a user shown as on-shift may be
deactivated.

To deactivate floor staff:




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/179.3.png)




i
Check the filters to verify deactivated users are not filtered out. Clear the Active
filter for deactivated users to appear.

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Floor Staff Information. The Floor Staff
Workbook window appears.

2\. Select the staff member to deactivate.

OR
Press and hold CTRL to select multiple staff members.

3\. Click Deactivate. The selected staff members are deactivated.


## 13.5 Promoting Floor Staff

An employee, or a group of employees, may be promoted up one level for the duration of a
shift. At the end of shift, the employees automatically revert to their regular title.

To temporarily promote floor staff:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Floor Staff Information. The Floor Staff
Workbook window appears.

2\. Select the staff member to promote.

OR
Press and hold CTRL to select multiple members.

3\. Click Promote. The Floor Staff Promotion window opens.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="179" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 13 Floor Staff Workbook" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/180.1.png)

4\. Select the promotion level in the Available Permission Groups pane. The Permissions
pane displays the permissions for the group.

5\. Click Assign. A confirmation prompt appears.

6\. Click Yes. The selected staff members are promoted.


## 13.6 Demoting Floor Staff

To demote floor staff:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Floor Staff Information. The Floor Staff
Workbook window appears.

2\. Select the staff member to demote.

OR
Press and hold CTRL to select multiple members.

3\. Click Demote. The employees are demoted to their previous level, and the updated Floor
Staff Workbook reappears.


## 13.7 On-Shift Status Change

To change a staff member's on-shift status:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Floor Staff Information. The Floor Staff
Workbook window appears.

2\. Select the staff member to place on shift
OR
Press and hold CTRL to select multiple members in the Floor Staff Workbook.

3\. Click On Shift. The Shift Change Time column displays the shift change date and time.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="180" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 13 Floor Staff Workbook" -->

The Status column indicates the employee is now on shift.

4\. Click Update. The selected staff members' information is highlighted in green.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/181.1.png)




Employee shifts are normally changed at the Attendant Workstation.


## 13.8 Off-Shift Status Change

To change a staff member's status to off-shift:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Floor Staff Information. The Floor Staff
Workbook window appears.

2\. Select the staff member to place off shift
OR
Press and hold CTRL to select multiple members.

3\. Click Off Shift. The Shift Change Time column displays the shift change date and time.
The Status column indicates the employee is now off shift.

4\. Confirm the status of all checked-out equipment.

5\. Click Update. The selected floor staff members' information is highlighted in white. The
shift information is removed from the Shift Change Time and Status columns.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/181.2.png)




Employee shifts are normally changed at the Attendant Workstation.


## 13.9 Setting Paging-Always On Shift

Use the Always On Shift option for employees who need to receive pages or e-mail notifications
for events occurring at any time of the day. Setting or clearing this feature takes effect
immediately.

To set the Always On Shift option for an employee:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Floor Staff Information. The Floor Staff
Workbook window appears.

2\. Select one of the following filter options:

· Active-Displays only active staff.

· Promoted-Displays only promoted staff.

· On Shift-Displays only staff currently on shift.

3\. Click Update. The staff list updates.

4\. Highlight the employee's name, then click Paging - Always On Shift. The user's Paging
Always On Shift field changes from N to Y.


## 13.10 Clearing Paging-Always On Shift

To remove the Always On Shift option for an employee:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Floor Staff Information. The Floor Staff
Workbook window appears.

2\. Select the Paging - Always On Shift filter.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="181" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 13 Floor Staff Workbook" -->

3\. Click Update.

4\. Highlight the employee name.

5\. Click Paging - Clear Always On. The user's Paging Always On Shift field changes from
Y to N.


### 13.11 Checking Out Equipment




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/182.1.png)




Any equipment an employee might use during the shift is normally issued at the
Attendant Workstation.

To check out equipment to a staff member:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Floor Staff Information. The Floor Staff
Workbook window appears.

2\. Select the staff member on shift (highlighted in green) in the Floor Staff Workbook to
receive issued equipment.

3\. Click Check Out. The Issue Equipment window appears.

If the staff member is not on shift, a confirmation dialog appears to place them on
shift. Click Yes; the staff member's status changes to on-shift.

4\. Select an equipment item, or press and hold CTRL to select multiple items.

5\. Click Accept. The equipment is issued to the staff member, and the updated Floor Staff
Workbook window reappears.


### 13.12 Checking In Equipment

To check in equipment from a staff member:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Floor Staff Information. The Floor Staff
Workbook window appears.

2\. Select the staff member in the Floor Staff Workbook to return equipment.

3\. Click Check In. The Check In Equipment window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/182.4.png)




i
The Check In Equipment window is identical to the Issue Equipment window. The
only difference is the title bar's wording.

4\. Select an equipment item OR press and hold CTRL to select multiple items.

5\. Click Accept. The equipment from the staff member is checked in, and the updated Floor
Staff Workbook window reappears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="182" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 13 Floor Staff Workbook" -->


## 13.13 Equipment Information

The Equipment Information feature tracks the status of each piece of equipment. Use the
Equipment Information window for all aspects of monitoring and maintaining equipment. Any
equipment an employee might use during the shift is normally issued at the Attendant
Workstation.

Supervisor functions covering equipment include the following:

· Adding, modifying, and deleting equipment

· Printing a barcode label for equipment

· Issuing equipment


## 13.13.1 Equipment Types

Use the Equipment Types feature to view, add, modify, and delete equipment types.


### 13.13.1.1 Viewing Equipment Types


#### To view equipment types:

Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Equipment Types OR click Edit Types in the
Equipment Information window. The Equipment Types window opens.

![Figure 13-1 Equipment Types](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/183.1.png)




#### 13.13.1.2 Adding Equipment Types


##### To add an equipment type:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Equipment Types OR click Edit Types in the
Equipment Information window. The Equipment Types window opens.

2\. Click Add in the Equipment Types window. The Equipment Type Update window
appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="183" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 13 Floor Staff Workbook" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/184.1.png)




3\. Enter the equipment type description in the Description field.

4\. Click the Type drop-down menu, then select an equipment type.

5\. Click Save. The equipment type is added.


##### 13.13.1.3 Issuing Equipment Types

To associate equipment types with staff members:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Equipment Types OR click Edit Types in the
Equipment Information window. The Equipment Types window opens.

2\. Select an equipment type in the Equipment Types window.

3\. Select a staff member in the Cannot Issue To pane, then click Add to make this
equipment type available to the staff member.

4\. Select a staff member in the Can Issue To pane, then click Remove to make the staff
member unable to receive this equipment type.

5\. Click Done. The staff members associated with the equipment type update.


##### 13.13.1.4 Modifying Equipment Types

To modify an equipment type:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Equipment Types OR click Edit Types in the
Equipment Information window. The Equipment Types window opens.

2\. Select an equipment type to modify in the Equipment Types window, then click Modify.
The Equipment Type Update window opens.

3\. Modify the equipment as needed.

4\. Click Save. The changes save.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="184" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 13 Floor Staff Workbook" -->


## 13.13.1.5 Deleting Equipment Types

To delete an equipment type:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Equipment Types OR click Edit Types in the
Equipment Information window. The Equipment Types window opens.

2\. Select an equipment type in the Equipment Types window, then click Delete. The
Equipment Types window no longer displays the deleted equipment type.

i
Confirm the equipment type being deleted as no confirmation message displays.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/185.1.png)




# 13.13.2 Accessing Equipment Information


## To access the Equipment Information window:

Click the Equipment icon on the Quick Access Toolbar OR navigate to IGT Spade > Floor
\> Equipment. The Equipment Information window appears.

Equipment items highlighted in yellow are currently checked out. Equipment items highlighted
in red have been checked out longer than eight hours.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/185.3.png)




i
Click a column heading to sort the data in ascending/descending order. If the
column width is set correctly, an arrow displays the sort direction.


# 13.13.3 Adding Equipment


## To add equipment:

1\. Click the Equipment icon on the Quick Access Toolbar OR navigate to IGT Spade >
Floor > Equipment. The Equipment Information window appears.

2\. Click Add. The Equipment Update window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/185.4.png)




<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="185" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 13 Floor Staff Workbook" -->

3\. Select a type from the Equipment Type drop-down list.

4\. Enter a barcode number for the equipment being added in the Barcode Number field.

5\. Enter a transmit ID number for the equipment in the Transmit ID field.

6\. Click Save. The equipment is added.


# 13.13.4 Modifying Equipment

To modify existing equipment:

1\. Click the Equipment icon on the Quick Access Toolbar OR navigate to IGT Spade >
Floor > Equipment. The Equipment Information window appears.

2\. Select specific equipment in the Equipment Information window.

3\. Click Modify. The Equipment Update window appears.

4\. Edit the applicable fields.

5\. Click Save. The updates save.


# 13.13.5 Deleting Equipment

To delete equipment:

1\. Click the Equipment icon on the Quick Access Toolbar OR navigate to IGT Spade >
Floor > Equipment. The Equipment Information window appears.

2\. Select specific equipment in the Equipment Information window.

3\. Click Delete. The equipment is deleted.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/186.1.png)




i
Confirm the equipment being deleted before clicking Delete. No delete confirmation
message appears.


# 13.13.6 Issuing Equipment




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/186.2.png)




Ž
Any equipment an employee might use during their shift is normally issued at the
Attendant Workstation.

To issue equipment:

1\. Click the Equipment icon on the Quick Access Toolbar OR navigate to IGT Spade >
Floor > Equipment. The Equipment Information window appears.

2\. Select the equipment to be issued, then click Issue. The Issue Equipment window
appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/186.3.png)




Ť
An Issue Warning message appears if the equipment is already checked out to
another staff member. Click OK to return to the Equipment Information window,
then check in the equipment for re-issue OR select another piece of equipment to
issue, then click Issue.

3\. Select the equipment to issue in the Issue Equipment window.

4\. Click Accept. The equipment is issued, and the Equipment Information window
reappears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="186" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 13 Floor Staff Workbook" -->


# 13.13.7 Printing Equipment Barcode Labels

To print a barcode label for an equipment item:

1\. Navigate to IGT Spade > Floor > Equipment OR click the Equipment icon on the
Quick Access Toolbar.The Equipment Information window appears.

2\. Select specific equipment needing a printed barcode label.

3\. Click Print Label. The attached barcode label printer prints the selected barcodes.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/187.1.png)




İ
A barcode label printer must be physically attached to the PC or available from the
network printers.

4\. Attach the label to the equipment. The label identifies the equipment when scanned
(issued/returned) at the Attendant Workstation.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="187" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 13 Floor Staff Workbook" -->
